title: Coelogyne cruciformis
taxonomy: Coelogyne cruciformis is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Coelogyneae. The Coelogyne cruciformis is further divided into two subspecies. It is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Thailand and Malaysia. The classification of Coelogyne cruciformis is based on its unique characteristics, including its distinctive cruciform (cross-shaped) flower petals.
uses: Coelogyne cruciformis is a species of orchid that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. The plant is reported to have various medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. Research suggests that extracts from Coelogyne cruciformis may have potential in treating certain health conditions, such as wound healing and skin infections. However, more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent. Further investigation is required to fully understand the plant's potential benefits and applications in medicine.

intro: Coelogyne cruciformis, also known as the cross orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, including wound healing and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne cruciformis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coelogyne cruciformis is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is commonly used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
